title: MCP Setup
description: Set up the moatlog MCP server so agents can read behavioral memory via get_task_context and related tools.

content.text: The Moatlog MCP server exposes behavioral memory as tools your agent can call. It reads moat.json from .moatlog/ and returns hot files, file histories, co-access patterns, and task-relevant context based on past agent sessions.

content.value: — top files by agent access frequency
— read/write counts, sessions, co-access for one path
— files opened in the same sessions as a given file
— files historically relevant to a task description

content.text: If you ran moatlog init, .cursor/mcp.json and .cursor/rules/moatlog.mdc are already configured. Restart Cursor after init so the MCP server connects.
To configure manually, add Moatlog to a project-level .cursor/mcp.json or your global ~/.cursor/mcp.json. The server must run with your project root as cwd so it finds .moatlog/moat.json.
If moatlog is not on your PATH, use the full path to the binary or npx:
For manual setup, add .cursor/rules/moatlog.mdc with alwaysApply: true so agents call get_task_context at session start.
Add Moatlog to your Claude Code MCP settings. The server uses stdio transport and reads moat.json from the current working directory.
Run Claude Code from your project root so the MCP server resolves .moatlog/ correctly. Claude Code agents should call get_task_context before starting significant work, same as Cursor.

content.description: Start the MCP server on stdio

content.text: Use this to test the server or debug tool responses. In normal use, your editor spawns this process automatically from MCP config.
If your agent reports that the Moatlog MCP server is missing or tools fail to connect, work through these checks in order.

content.value: — project-level .cursor/mcp.json often points at packages/cli/dist/bin.js. Run
in MCP config if you installed from npm).
— MCP servers load at startup. After changing mcp.json or building the binary, restart Cursor or reload the moatlog server in Settings → MCP.
— in Cursor Settings → MCP, Moatlog should show as connected. If it failed on launch, expand the entry for the error (missing binary, wrong cwd, etc.).
. You should see "moatlog MCP server running" on stderr. If that works but Cursor still fails, the issue is editor config, not the server.
— after reconnecting MCP, open a fresh agent session so tools are available to the agent.

content.title: Run moatlog distill before starting a session for best results
content.text: MCP tools read moat.json first. If events have accumulated since the last distill, run moatlog distill (or let the stop hook handle it) so get_task_context returns current hot files and prompt windows.
